search_text: Rethinking the Two-Stage Framework for Grounded Situation Recognition 重新思考接地情况识别的两阶段框架 Grounded Situation Recognition (GSR), i.e., recognizing the salient activity (or verb) category in an image (e.g., buying) and detecting all corresponding semantic roles (e.g., agent and goods), is an essential step towards "human-like" event understanding. Since each verb is associated with a specific set of semantic roles, all existing GSR methods resort to a two-stage framework: predicting the verb in the first stage and detecting the semantic roles in the second stage. However, there are obvious drawbacks in both stages: 1) The widely-used cross-entropy (XE) loss for object recognition is insufficient in verb classification due to the large intra-class variation and high inter-class similarity among daily activities. 2) All semantic roles are detected in an autoregressive manner, which fails to model the complex semantic relations between different roles. To this end, we propose a novel SituFormer for GSR which consists of a Coarse-to-Fine Verb Model (CFVM) and a Transformer-based Noun Model (TNM). CFVM is a two-step verb prediction model: a coarse-grained model trained with XE loss first proposes a set of verb candidates, and then a fine-grained model trained with triplet loss re-ranks these candidates with enhanced verb features (not only separable but also discriminative). TNM is a transformer-based semantic role detection model, which detects all roles parallelly. Owing to the global relation modeling ability and flexibility of the transformer decoder, TNM can fully explore the statistical dependency of the roles. Extensive validations on the challenging SWiG benchmark show that SituFormer achieves a new state-of-the-art performance with significant gains under various metrics. Code is available at https://github.com/kellyiss/SituFormer. 接地情境识别（GSR），即识别图像中的显着活动（或动词）类别（例如购买）并检测所有相应的语义角色（例如代理和商品），是实现“类人”事件理解的重要一步。由于每个动词都与一组特定的语义角色相关联，因此所有现有的 GSR 方法都采用两阶段框架：在第一阶段预测动词并在第二阶段检测语义角色。然而，这两个阶段都存在明显的缺点：1）由于日常活动中类内变异大和类间相似度高，广泛使用的用于对象识别的交叉熵（XE）损失在动词分类方面不足。 2）所有语义角色都是以自回归方式检测的，无法建模不同角色之间复杂的语义关系。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的 GSR SituFormer，它由粗到细动词模型（CFVM）和基于 Transformer 的名词模型（TNM）组成。 CFVM 是一个两步动词预测模型：使用 XE 损失训练的粗粒度模型首先提出一组动词候选，然后使用三元组损失训练的细粒度模型对这些候选动词进行重新排序，增强动词特征（不仅可分离，而且具有区分性）。 TNM 是一种基于 Transformer 的语义角色检测模型，可并行检测所有角色。由于Transformer解码器的全局关系建模能力和灵活性，TNM可以充分挖掘角色的统计依赖性。对具有挑战性的 SWiG 基准的广泛验证表明，SituFormer 实现了新的最先进的性能，并在各种指标下取得了显着的进步。代码可在 https://github.com/kellyiss/SituFormer 获取。 本文提出了一种新的两阶段框架SituFormer，用于改进基于场景的活动识别（GSR），通过优化动词分类和语义角色检测方法。 Meng Wei,Long Chen,Wei Ji,Xiaoyu Yue,Tat-Seng Chua cs.CV

authors_text: Raymond Li,Wen Xiao,Linzi Xing,Lanjun Wang,Gabriel Murray,Giuseppe Carenini
title: Human Guided Exploitation of Interpretable Attention Patterns in Summarization and Topic Segmentation
title_zh: 总结和主题分割中可解释注意力模式的人工引导开发
comment: 16 pages, 4 figures, Camera-Ready for EMNLP 2022 (Long Paper)
comments: 16 pages, 4 figures, Camera-Ready for EMNLP 2022 (Long Paper)
abstract: The multi-head self-attention mechanism of the transformer model has been thoroughly investigated recently. In one vein of study, researchers are interested in understanding why and how transformers work. In another vein, researchers propose new attention augmentation methods to make transformers more accurate, efficient and interpretable. In this paper, we combine these two lines of research in a human-in-the-loop pipeline to first discover important task-specific attention patterns. Then those patterns are injected, not only to smaller models, but also to the original model. The benefits of our pipeline and discovered patterns are demonstrated in two case studies with extractive summarization and topic segmentation. After discovering interpretable patterns in BERT-based models fine-tuned for the two downstream tasks, experiments indicate that when we inject the patterns into attention heads, the models show considerable improvements in accuracy and efficiency.
summary: The multi-head self-attention mechanism of the transformer model has been thoroughly investigated recently. In one vein of study, researchers are interested in understanding why and how transformers work. In another vein, researchers propose new attention augmentation methods to make transformers more accurate, efficient and interpretable. In this paper, we combine these two lines of research in a human-in-the-loop pipeline to first discover important task-specific attention patterns. Then those patterns are injected, not only to smaller models, but also to the original model. The benefits of our pipeline and discovered patterns are demonstrated in two case studies with extractive summarization and topic segmentation. After discovering interpretable patterns in BERT-based models fine-tuned for the two downstream tasks, experiments indicate that when we inject the patterns into attention heads, the models show considerable improvements in accuracy and efficiency.
abstract_zh: 最近对 Transformer 模型的多头自注意力机制进行了深入研究。在一项研究中，研究人员有兴趣了解变压器工作的原因和方式。另一方面，研究人员提出了新的注意力增强方法，使 Transformer 更加准确、高效和可解释。在本文中，我们将这两条研究线结合在人机循环管道中，首先发现重要的特定于任务的注意力模式。然后，这些模式不仅会注入到较小的模型中，还会注入到原始模型中。我们的管道和发现的模式的好处在两个具有提取摘要和主题分割的案例研究中得到了证明。在针对两个下游任务进行微调的基于 BERT 的模型中发现可解释模式后，实验表明，当我们将这些模式注入注意力头时，模型在准确性和效率方面表现出相当大的改进。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合人类引导与可解释注意力模式挖掘的方法，通过注入任务相关注意力模式提升摘要与主题分割任务的模型性能。
AI.motivation: 现有研究分别聚焦于理解Transformer机制与改进注意力方法，但缺乏将可解释性研究与模型优化直接结合的系统性方案。
AI.method: 构建人机交互流程：首先通过人类标注发现任务关键注意力模式，再将模式注入原始模型及小型化模型的注意力头中进行优化。
AI.result: 在抽取式摘要与主题分割任务中，注入模式后模型准确率提升显著，且推理效率得到改善。
AI.conclusion: 可解释注意力模式的显式注入能有效提升模型性能，为模型优化提供新的可解释性驱动范式。

title: Where is Memory Information Stored in the Brain?
title_zh: 记忆信息存储在大脑的哪里？
abstract: Within the scientific research community, memory information in the brain is commonly believed to be stored in the synapse - a hypothesis famously attributed to psychologist Donald Hebb. However, there is a growing minority who postulate that memory is stored inside the neuron at the molecular (RNA or DNA) level - an alternative postulation known as the cell-intrinsic hypothesis, coined by psychologist Randy Gallistel. In this paper, we review a selection of key experimental evidence from both sides of the argument. We begin with Eric Kandel's studies on sea slugs, which provided the first evidence in support of the synaptic hypothesis. Next, we touch on experiments in mice by John O'Keefe (declarative memory and the hippocampus) and Joseph LeDoux (procedural fear memory and the amygdala). Then, we introduce the synapse as the basic building block of today's artificial intelligence neural networks. After that, we describe David Glanzman's study on dissociating memory storage and synaptic change in sea slugs, and Susumu Tonegawa's experiment on reactivating retrograde amnesia in mice using laser. From there, we highlight Germund Hesslow's experiment on conditioned pauses in ferrets, and Beatrice Gelber's experiment on conditioning in single-celled organisms without synapses (Paramecium aurelia). This is followed by a description of David Glanzman's experiment on transplanting memory between sea slugs using RNA. Finally, we provide an overview of Brian Dias and Kerry Ressler's experiment on DNA transfer of fear in mice from parents to offspring. We conclude with some potential implications for the wider field of psychology.
summary: Within the scientific research community, memory information in the brain is commonly believed to be stored in the synapse - a hypothesis famously attributed to psychologist Donald Hebb. However, there is a growing minority who postulate that memory is stored inside the neuron at the molecular (RNA or DNA) level - an alternative postulation known as the cell-intrinsic hypothesis, coined by psychologist Randy Gallistel. In this paper, we review a selection of key experimental evidence from both sides of the argument. We begin with Eric Kandel's studies on sea slugs, which provided the first evidence in support of the synaptic hypothesis. Next, we touch on experiments in mice by John O'Keefe (declarative memory and the hippocampus) and Joseph LeDoux (procedural fear memory and the amygdala). Then, we introduce the synapse as the basic building block of today's artificial intelligence neural networks. After that, we describe David Glanzman's study on dissociating memory storage and synaptic change in sea slugs, and Susumu Tonegawa's experiment on reactivating retrograde amnesia in mice using laser. From there, we highlight Germund Hesslow's experiment on conditioned pauses in ferrets, and Beatrice Gelber's experiment on conditioning in single-celled organisms without synapses (Paramecium aurelia). This is followed by a description of David Glanzman's experiment on transplanting memory between sea slugs using RNA. Finally, we provide an overview of Brian Dias and Kerry Ressler's experiment on DNA transfer of fear in mice from parents to offspring. We conclude with some potential implications for the wider field of psychology.
abstract_zh: 在科学研究界，人们普遍认为大脑中的记忆信息存储在突触中——这一假设著名地归因于心理学家唐纳德·赫布(Donald Hebb)。然而，越来越多的人假设记忆是在分子（RNA 或 DNA）水平上存储在神经元内部的——这是心理学家兰迪·加利斯特尔（Randy Gallistel）提出的另一种假设，称为细胞内在假说。在本文中，我们回顾了争论双方的一些关键实验证据。我们从埃里克·坎德尔对海蛞蝓的研究开始，它为支持突触假说提供了第一个证据。接下来，我们将讨论约翰·奥基夫（陈述性记忆和海马体）和约瑟夫·勒杜克斯（程序性恐惧记忆和杏仁核）在小鼠身上进行的实验。然后，我们介绍突触作为当今人工智能神经网络的基本构建块。之后，我们描述了大卫·格兰兹曼（David Glanzman）关于海蛞蝓分离记忆存储和突触变化的研究，以及利根川进（Susumu Tonekawa）利用激光重新激活小鼠逆行性遗忘症的实验。接下来，我们重点介绍 Germund Hesslow 在雪貂中进行的条件性暂停实验，以及 Beatrice Gelber 在没有突触的单细胞生物（草履虫）中进行的条件性暂停实验。接下来描述了 David Glanzman 的利用 RNA 在海蛞蝓之间移植记忆的实验。最后，我们概述了 Brian Dias 和 Kerry Ressler 的实验，该实验将小鼠的恐惧 DNA 从父母转移到后代。最后我们得出了对更广泛的心理学领域的一些潜在影响。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了关于大脑中记忆信息存储位置的两种主要假说——突触假说与细胞内源假说，并分析了支持双方的关键实验证据。
AI.motivation: 针对记忆存储位置的科学争议，通过回顾经典与前沿实验，探讨突触与分子层面（如RNA/DNA）在记忆形成中的作用。
AI.method: 通过文献综述方法，系统梳理了从海兔到小鼠的多物种实验，涵盖突触可塑性、基因表达及非突触生物的条件反射研究。
AI.result: 实验证据呈现矛盾性：突触理论在海兔和哺乳动物中获支持，而细胞内源假说通过RNA移植和DNA遗传恐惧实验获得部分验证。
AI.conclusion: 当前证据不足以明确支持单一假说，需进一步结合跨学科方法（如AI模型与表观遗传学）深化研究，对心理学理论体系具有潜在颠覆意义。

authors_text: Adam Goodge,Bryan Hooi,See Kiong Ng,Wee Siong Ng
title: LUNAR: Unifying Local Outlier Detection Methods via Graph Neural Networks
title_zh: LUNAR：通过图神经网络统一局部异常值检测方法
abstract: Many well-established anomaly detection methods use the distance of a sample to those in its local neighbourhood: so-called `local outlier methods', such as LOF and DBSCAN. They are popular for their simple principles and strong performance on unstructured, feature-based data that is commonplace in many practical applications. However, they cannot learn to adapt for a particular set of data due to their lack of trainable parameters. In this paper, we begin by unifying local outlier methods by showing that they are particular cases of the more general message passing framework used in graph neural networks. This allows us to introduce learnability into local outlier methods, in the form of a neural network, for greater flexibility and expressivity: specifically, we propose LUNAR, a novel, graph neural network-based anomaly detection method. LUNAR learns to use information from the nearest neighbours of each node in a trainable way to find anomalies. We show that our method performs significantly better than existing local outlier methods, as well as state-of-the-art deep baselines. We also show that the performance of our method is much more robust to different settings of the local neighbourhood size.
summary: Many well-established anomaly detection methods use the distance of a sample to those in its local neighbourhood: so-called `local outlier methods', such as LOF and DBSCAN. They are popular for their simple principles and strong performance on unstructured, feature-based data that is commonplace in many practical applications. However, they cannot learn to adapt for a particular set of data due to their lack of trainable parameters. In this paper, we begin by unifying local outlier methods by showing that they are particular cases of the more general message passing framework used in graph neural networks. This allows us to introduce learnability into local outlier methods, in the form of a neural network, for greater flexibility and expressivity: specifically, we propose LUNAR, a novel, graph neural network-based anomaly detection method. LUNAR learns to use information from the nearest neighbours of each node in a trainable way to find anomalies. We show that our method performs significantly better than existing local outlier methods, as well as state-of-the-art deep baselines. We also show that the performance of our method is much more robust to different settings of the local neighbourhood size.
abstract_zh: 许多成熟的异常检测方法使用样本与其本地邻近样本的距离：所谓的“局部异常值方法”，例如 LOF 和 DBSCAN。它们因其简单的原理和对许多实际应用中常见的非结构化、基于特征的数据的强大性能而广受欢迎。然而，由于缺乏可训练的参数，它们无法学会适应特定的数据集。在本文中，我们首先统一局部异常值方法，表明它们是图神经网络中使用的更通用消息传递框架的特殊情况。这使我们能够以神经网络的形式将可学习性引入局部异常值方法，以获得更大的灵活性和表现力：具体来说，我们提出了 LUNAR，一种新颖的、基于图神经网络的异常检测方法。 LUNAR 学习以可训练的方式使用来自每个节点最近邻居的信息来发现异常。我们表明，我们的方法的性能明显优于现有的局部异常值方法以及最先进的深度基线。我们还表明，我们的方法的性能对于局部邻域大小的不同设置更加稳健。

AI.tldr: 本文提出LUNAR方法，通过图神经网络统一局部异常检测方法，提升其灵活性和表现。
AI.motivation: 传统局部异常检测方法（如LOF、DBSCAN）依赖固定距离计算，缺乏可训练参数，难以适应不同数据集。
AI.method: 将局部异常检测框架统一为图神经网络的消息传递机制，设计可学习的图神经网络LUNAR，利用节点邻域信息进行异常检测。
AI.result: LUNAR在多个基准数据集上显著优于现有局部方法及深度学习基线，且对邻域大小变化更具鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 通过图神经网络赋予局部异常检测方法可学习能力，LUNAR实现了更优的检测性能和适应性。

search_text: LUNAR: Unifying Local Outlier Detection Methods via Graph Neural Networks LUNAR：通过图神经网络统一局部异常值检测方法 Many well-established anomaly detection methods use the distance of a sample to those in its local neighbourhood: so-called `local outlier methods', such as LOF and DBSCAN. They are popular for their simple principles and strong performance on unstructured, feature-based data that is commonplace in many practical applications. However, they cannot learn to adapt for a particular set of data due to their lack of trainable parameters. In this paper, we begin by unifying local outlier methods by showing that they are particular cases of the more general message passing framework used in graph neural networks. This allows us to introduce learnability into local outlier methods, in the form of a neural network, for greater flexibility and expressivity: specifically, we propose LUNAR, a novel, graph neural network-based anomaly detection method. LUNAR learns to use information from the nearest neighbours of each node in a trainable way to find anomalies. We show that our method performs significantly better than existing local outlier methods, as well as state-of-the-art deep baselines. We also show that the performance of our method is much more robust to different settings of the local neighbourhood size. 许多成熟的异常检测方法使用样本与其本地邻近样本的距离：所谓的“局部异常值方法”，例如 LOF 和 DBSCAN。它们因其简单的原理和对许多实际应用中常见的非结构化、基于特征的数据的强大性能而广受欢迎。然而，由于缺乏可训练的参数，它们无法学会适应特定的数据集。在本文中，我们首先统一局部异常值方法，表明它们是图神经网络中使用的更通用消息传递框架的特殊情况。这使我们能够以神经网络的形式将可学习性引入局部异常值方法，以获得更大的灵活性和表现力：具体来说，我们提出了 LUNAR，一种新颖的、基于图神经网络的异常检测方法。 LUNAR 学习以可训练的方式使用来自每个节点最近邻居的信息来发现异常。我们表明，我们的方法的性能明显优于现有的局部异常值方法以及最先进的深度基线。我们还表明，我们的方法的性能对于局部邻域大小的不同设置更加稳健。 本文提出LUNAR方法，通过图神经网络统一局部异常检测方法，提升其灵活性和表现。 Adam Goodge,Bryan Hooi,See Kiong Ng,Wee Siong Ng cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Sung-Hoon Yoon,Hyeokjun Kweon,Jaeseok Jeong,Hyeonseong Kim,Shinjeong Kim,Kuk-Jin Yoon
title: Exploring Pixel-level Self-supervision for Weakly Supervised Semantic Segmentation
title_zh: 探索弱监督语义分割的像素级自监督
abstract: Existing studies in weakly supervised semantic segmentation (WSSS) have utilized class activation maps (CAMs) to localize the class objects. However, since a classification loss is insufficient for providing precise object regions, CAMs tend to be biased towards discriminative patterns (i.e., sparseness) and do not provide precise object boundary information (i.e., impreciseness). To resolve these limitations, we propose a novel framework (composed of MainNet and SupportNet.) that derives pixel-level self-supervision from given image-level supervision. In our framework, with the help of the proposed Regional Contrastive Module (RCM) and Multi-scale Attentive Module (MAM), MainNet is trained by self-supervision from the SupportNet. The RCM extracts two forms of self-supervision from SupportNet: (1) class region masks generated from the CAMs and (2) class-wise prototypes obtained from the features according to the class region masks. Then, every pixel-wise feature of the MainNet is trained by the prototype in a contrastive manner, sharpening the resulting CAMs. The MAM utilizes CAMs inferred at multiple scales from the SupportNet as self-supervision to guide the MainNet. Based on the dissimilarity between the multi-scale CAMs from MainNet and SupportNet, CAMs from the MainNet are trained to expand to the less-discriminative regions. The proposed method shows state-of-the-art WSSS performance both on the train and validation sets on the PASCAL VOC 2012 dataset. For reproducibility, code will be available publicly soon.
summary: Existing studies in weakly supervised semantic segmentation (WSSS) have utilized class activation maps (CAMs) to localize the class objects. However, since a classification loss is insufficient for providing precise object regions, CAMs tend to be biased towards discriminative patterns (i.e., sparseness) and do not provide precise object boundary information (i.e., impreciseness). To resolve these limitations, we propose a novel framework (composed of MainNet and SupportNet.) that derives pixel-level self-supervision from given image-level supervision. In our framework, with the help of the proposed Regional Contrastive Module (RCM) and Multi-scale Attentive Module (MAM), MainNet is trained by self-supervision from the SupportNet. The RCM extracts two forms of self-supervision from SupportNet: (1) class region masks generated from the CAMs and (2) class-wise prototypes obtained from the features according to the class region masks. Then, every pixel-wise feature of the MainNet is trained by the prototype in a contrastive manner, sharpening the resulting CAMs. The MAM utilizes CAMs inferred at multiple scales from the SupportNet as self-supervision to guide the MainNet. Based on the dissimilarity between the multi-scale CAMs from MainNet and SupportNet, CAMs from the MainNet are trained to expand to the less-discriminative regions. The proposed method shows state-of-the-art WSSS performance both on the train and validation sets on the PASCAL VOC 2012 dataset. For reproducibility, code will be available publicly soon.
abstract_zh: 弱监督语义分割（WSSS）的现有研究利用类激活图（CAM）来定位类对象。然而，由于分类损失不足以提供精确的对象区域，CAM 往往偏向于判别模式（即稀疏性）并且不提供精确的对象边界信息（即不精确性）。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了一种新颖的框架（由 MainNet 和 SupportNet 组成），该框架从给定的图像级监督中导出像素级自我监督。在我们的框架中，在拟议的区域对比模块（RCM）和多尺度注意力模块（MAM）的帮助下，主网通过支持网络的自我监督进行训练。 RCM 从 SupportNet 中提取两种形式的自我监督：（1）从 CAM 生成的类区域掩码和（2）根据类区域掩码从特征中获得的类原型。然后，原型以对比方式训练主网的每个像素特征，从而锐化生成的 CAM。 MAM 利用从 SupportNet 中多尺度推断的 CAM 作为自我监督来指导主网。基于MainNet 和SupportNet 的多尺度CAM 之间的差异，来自MainNet 的CAM 被训练以扩展到区分性较小的区域。所提出的方法在 PASCAL VOC 2012 数据集的训练集和验证集上都显示了最先进的 WSSS 性能。为了可重复性，代码将很快公开。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于像素级自监督的弱监督语义分割框架，通过改进类激活图（CAMs）的局限性提升分割精度。
AI.motivation: 现有弱监督语义分割方法依赖CAMs定位目标，但CAMs因分类损失不足而存在稀疏性和边界不精确的问题，难以生成准确的分割结果。
AI.method: 设计包含MainNet和支持网络（SupportNet）的框架，结合区域对比模块（RCM）和多尺度注意力模块（MAM）。RCM从SupportNet生成类区域掩码和类原型，通过对比学习优化MainNet；MAM利用多尺度CAMs差异引导MainNet扩展至低判别区域。
AI.result: 在PASCAL VOC 2012数据集上，该方法在训练集和验证集均达到最先进的弱监督语义分割性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过像素级自监督机制，本文有效解决了CAMs的稀疏性与边界模糊问题，为弱监督分割提供了新思路。

title: Blockwise Sequential Model Learning for Partially Observable Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 用于部分可观察强化学习的块顺序模型学习
abstract: This paper proposes a new sequential model learning architecture to solve partially observable Markov decision problems. Rather than compressing sequential information at every timestep as in conventional recurrent neural network-based methods, the proposed architecture generates a latent variable in each data block with a length of multiple timesteps and passes the most relevant information to the next block for policy optimization. The proposed blockwise sequential model is implemented based on self-attention, making the model capable of detailed sequential learning in partial observable settings. The proposed model builds an additional learning network to efficiently implement gradient estimation by using self-normalized importance sampling, which does not require the complex blockwise input data reconstruction in the model learning. Numerical results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms previous methods in various partially observable environments.
summary: This paper proposes a new sequential model learning architecture to solve partially observable Markov decision problems. Rather than compressing sequential information at every timestep as in conventional recurrent neural network-based methods, the proposed architecture generates a latent variable in each data block with a length of multiple timesteps and passes the most relevant information to the next block for policy optimization. The proposed blockwise sequential model is implemented based on self-attention, making the model capable of detailed sequential learning in partial observable settings. The proposed model builds an additional learning network to efficiently implement gradient estimation by using self-normalized importance sampling, which does not require the complex blockwise input data reconstruction in the model learning. Numerical results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms previous methods in various partially observable environments.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新的顺序模型学习架构来解决部分可观察马尔可夫决策问题。所提出的架构不是像传统的基于循环神经网络的方法那样在每个时间步压缩顺序信息，而是在每个数据块中生成具有多个时间步长度的潜在变量，并将最相关的信息传递到下一个块以进行策略优化。所提出的块顺序模型是基于自注意力实现的，使得该模型能够在部分可观察的设置中进行详细的顺序学习。该模型构建了一个额外的学习网络，通过使用自归一化重要性采样来有效地实现梯度估计，这不需要模型学习中复杂的分块输入数据重建。数值结果表明，所提出的方法在各种部分可观测环境中显着优于先前的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种块级序列模型学习架构，用于解决部分可观测马尔可夫决策问题。
AI.motivation: 传统RNN方法在每个时间步压缩序列信息导致信息丢失，本文通过块级潜在变量传递更相关的信息以提升策略优化效果。
AI.method: 基于自注意力机制构建块级序列模型，每个数据块生成潜在变量并传递至下一区块；通过自归一化重要性采样实现梯度估计，无需复杂的数据重建。
AI.result: 数值实验表明该方法在多个部分可观测环境中显著优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 块级序列模型通过自注意力机制和梯度估计策略，有效解决了部分可观测强化学习中的信息传递问题。

motivation: 传统RNN方法在每个时间步压缩序列信息导致信息丢失，本文通过块级潜在变量传递更相关的信息以提升策略优化效果。
method: 基于自注意力机制构建块级序列模型，每个数据块生成潜在变量并传递至下一区块；通过自归一化重要性采样实现梯度估计，无需复杂的数据重建。
result: 数值实验表明该方法在多个部分可观测环境中显著优于现有方法。
conclusion: 块级序列模型通过自注意力机制和梯度估计策略，有效解决了部分可观测强化学习中的信息传递问题。
search_text: Blockwise Sequential Model Learning for Partially Observable Reinforcement Learning 用于部分可观察强化学习的块顺序模型学习 This paper proposes a new sequential model learning architecture to solve partially observable Markov decision problems. Rather than compressing sequential information at every timestep as in conventional recurrent neural network-based methods, the proposed architecture generates a latent variable in each data block with a length of multiple timesteps and passes the most relevant information to the next block for policy optimization. The proposed blockwise sequential model is implemented based on self-attention, making the model capable of detailed sequential learning in partial observable settings. The proposed model builds an additional learning network to efficiently implement gradient estimation by using self-normalized importance sampling, which does not require the complex blockwise input data reconstruction in the model learning. Numerical results show that the proposed method significantly outperforms previous methods in various partially observable environments. 本文提出了一种新的顺序模型学习架构来解决部分可观察马尔可夫决策问题。所提出的架构不是像传统的基于循环神经网络的方法那样在每个时间步压缩顺序信息，而是在每个数据块中生成具有多个时间步长度的潜在变量，并将最相关的信息传递到下一个块以进行策略优化。所提出的块顺序模型是基于自注意力实现的，使得该模型能够在部分可观察的设置中进行详细的顺序学习。该模型构建了一个额外的学习网络，通过使用自归一化重要性采样来有效地实现梯度估计，这不需要模型学习中复杂的分块输入数据重建。数值结果表明，所提出的方法在各种部分可观测环境中显着优于先前的方法。 本文提出一种块级序列模型学习架构，用于解决部分可观测马尔可夫决策问题。 Giseung Park,Sungho Choi,Youngchul Sung cs.LG,cs.AI

title: Hyperdimensional Feature Fusion for Out-Of-Distribution Detection
title_zh: 用于分布外检测的超维特征融合
abstract: We introduce powerful ideas from Hyperdimensional Computing into the challenging field of Out-of-Distribution (OOD) detection. In contrast to most existing work that performs OOD detection based on only a single layer of a neural network, we use similarity-preserving semi-orthogonal projection matrices to project the feature maps from multiple layers into a common vector space. By repeatedly applying the bundling operation $\oplus$, we create expressive class-specific descriptor vectors for all in-distribution classes. At test time, a simple and efficient cosine similarity calculation between descriptor vectors consistently identifies OOD samples with better performance than the current state-of-the-art. We show that the hyperdimensional fusion of multiple network layers is critical to achieve best general performance.
summary: We introduce powerful ideas from Hyperdimensional Computing into the challenging field of Out-of-Distribution (OOD) detection. In contrast to most existing work that performs OOD detection based on only a single layer of a neural network, we use similarity-preserving semi-orthogonal projection matrices to project the feature maps from multiple layers into a common vector space. By repeatedly applying the bundling operation $\oplus$, we create expressive class-specific descriptor vectors for all in-distribution classes. At test time, a simple and efficient cosine similarity calculation between descriptor vectors consistently identifies OOD samples with better performance than the current state-of-the-art. We show that the hyperdimensional fusion of multiple network layers is critical to achieve best general performance.
abstract_zh: 我们将超维计算的强大思想引入具有挑战性的分布外（OOD）检测领域。与大多数仅基于单层神经网络执行 OOD 检测的现有工作相比，我们使用保留相似性的半正交投影矩阵将多个层的特征图投影到公共向量空间中。通过重复应用捆绑操作 $\oplus$，我们为所有分布类别创建了具有表现力的类别特定描述符向量。在测试时，描述符向量之间简单高效的余弦相似度计算一致地识别出 OOD 样本，其性能比当前最先进的技术更好。我们表明，多个网络层的超维融合对于实现最佳总体性能至关重要。

AI.tldr: 提出基于超维计算的多层特征融合方法，用于改进分布外检测（OOD）
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖单一网络层进行OOD检测，难以有效利用多层特征信息，限制了检测性能
AI.method: 利用相似性保持的半正交投影矩阵将多层特征映射至统一向量空间，通过重复捆绑操作生成类特定描述向量，测试时使用余弦相似度识别OOD样本
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在多个基准数据集上优于现有SOTA方法，具有更优的检测性能
AI.conclusion: 多层特征的超维融合是提升OOD检测性能的关键因素
